quote: dalīl-e-sub.h-e-raushan hai sitāroñ kī tunuk-tābī / ufuq se āftāb ubhrā gayā daur-e-girāñ-ḳhvābī
translit: dalil-e-subh-e-raushan hai sitaron ki tunuk-tabi / ufuq se aftab ubhra gaya daur-e-girankhwabi
translation: Proof of bright morning is the stars' fading light; the sun has risen from the horizon, the age of heavy slumber is past.
annot: subh-e-raushan — bright morning as sign of a new era
title: Tulu-e-Islam (Bang-e-Dara, poem 163)
author: Allama Iqbal

quote: qalb o nazar kī zindagī dasht meñ sub.h kā samāñ / chashma-e-āftāb se nuur kī naddiyāñ ravāñ
translit: qalb o nazar ki zindagi dasht mein subh ka saman / chashma-e-aftab se nur ki nadiyan ravan
translation: Life of heart and sight — in the desert, like the first light of morning; from the sun's spring, rivers of light flowing free.
annot: subh — desert dawn as spiritual renewal
title: Zauq-o-Shauq (Kulliyat-e-Iqbal)
author: Allama Iqbal

quote: jab tujhe yaad kar liyā subh mahak mahak uThī / jab terā ġham jagā to raat tarap tarap ke so ga.ī
translit: jab tujhe yaad kar liya subh mahak mahak uthi / jab tera gham jaga to raat tarap tarap ke so gai
translation: When your memory stirred, the morning bloomed fragrant; when your sorrow woke, the night wept itself to sleep.
annot: subah — when morning is infused with the memory of the beloved
title: Ghazal (Kulliyat)
author: Faiz Ahmad Faiz

quote: ye dāġh dāġh ujālā ye shab-gazīda sahar / vo intizār thā jis kā ye vo sahar to nahīñ
translit: ye daag daag ujaala ye shab-gazida sahar / vo intizar tha jis ka ye vo sahar to nahin
translation: This blemished, pock-marked light, this night-bitten dawn — this is not the dawn we waited for.
annot: sahar — the wounded dawn of independence
title: Subh-e-Azadi (August-47) — Naqsh-e-Faryadi / Dast-e-Saba
author: Faiz Ahmad Faiz

quote: vo subh kabhī to ā.egī / vo subh kabhī to ā.egī
translit: vo subh kabhi to aayegi / vo subh kabhi to aayegi
translation: That morning will surely come, that morning will surely come.
annot: subah — the morning that will surely come (refrain)
title: Vo Subh Kabhi To Aayegi — Kulliyat-e-Sahir Ludhianvi (p. 342)
author: Sahir Ludhianvi

quote: nā.ī sub.h par nazar hai magar āh ye bhī Dar hai / ye sahar bhī rafta rafta kahīñ shaam tak na pahuñche
translit: nai subh par nazar hai magar aah ye bhi dar hai / ye sahar bhi rafta rafta kahin sham tak na pahunche
translation: My gaze is fixed on the new morning, but there is this fear too — that even this dawn may, little by little, reach evening.
annot: subh / sahar — the new dawn feared may itself become dusk
title: Ghazal (Filmi Shayari)
author: Shakeel Badayuni

quote: dopahar kī dhuup meñ mere bulāne ke liye / vo tirā koThe pe nañge paañv aanā yaad hai
translit: dopahar ki dhup mein mere bulane ke liye / vo tera kothe pe nange paon aana yaad hai
translation: To answer my call in the blazing midday sun — I remember how you came barefoot to the rooftop.
annot: dopahar — barefoot on the rooftop in the midday heat
title: Paiman-e-Gazal-Avval (p. 265)
author: Hasrat Mohani

quote: shikva-e-zulmat-e-shab se to kahīñ behtar thā / apne hisse kī koī sham.a jalāte jaate
translit: shikva-e-zulmat-e-shab se to kahin behtar tha / apne hisse ki koi shama jalate jaate
translation: Far better than complaining of the night's darkness — to have lit at least one candle of your own share and gone.
annot: shab / shama — better to light your own candle than complain of the dark
title: Ghazal
author: Ahmad Faraz

quote: sāqiyā ek nazar jaam se pahle pahle / ham ko jaanā hai kahīñ shaam se pahle pahle
translit: saqiya ek nazar jaam se pahle pahle / ham ko jana hai kahin sham se pahle pahle
translation: Cupbearer, one glance — before the cup; we must depart somewhere before evening.
annot: sham — we must be gone somewhere before evening falls
title: Ghazal
author: Ahmad Faraz

quote: us ne sukūt-e-shab meñ bhī apnā payām rakh diyā / hijr kī raat baam par māh-e-tamām rakh diyā
translit: us ne sukut-e-shab mein bhi apna payam rakh diya / hijr ki raat baam par mah-e-tamam rakh diya
translation: Even in the silence of night, they left their message — on the rooftop of separation's night, they placed a full moon.
annot: shab / raat — even in night's silence, a message was left; a full moon placed on the rooftop of separation
title: Ghazal (Editors' Choice — Rekhta)
author: Ahmad Faraz

quote: dil nā-umīd to nahīñ nākām hī to hai / lambī hai ġham kī shaam magar shaam hī to hai
translit: dil na-umid to nahin nakaam hi to hai / lambi hai gham ki sham magar sham hi to hai
translation: The heart is not without hope — it has merely failed; the evening of sorrow is long, but it is only an evening.
annot: sham — the long evening of grief is still only an evening
title: Ghazal (Top 10 Rekhta selection)
author: Faiz Ahmad Faiz

quote: shām-e-firāq ab na pūchh aa.ī aur aa ke Tal ga.ī / dil thā ki phir bahal gayā jaañ thī ki phir sambhal ga.ī
translit: sham-e-firaq ab na puchh aai aur aa ke tal gai / dil tha ki phir bahal gaya jan thi ki phir sambhal gai
translation: Ask no more of this evening of separation — it came and somehow passed; the heart found distraction, the soul found footing again.
annot: sham-e-firaq — the evening of separation came and slipped away
title: Ghazal
author: Faiz Ahmad Faiz

quote: shām bhī thī dhuāñ dhuāñ husn bhī thā udaas udaas / dil ko ka.ī kahāniyāñ yaad sī aa ke rah ga.iiñ
translit: sham bhi thi dhuan dhuan husn bhi tha udas udas / dil ko kai kahaniyan yaad si aa ke rah gain
translation: The evening was smoky and dim, beauty itself appeared sad; many stories rose in the heart, half-remembered, and stayed.
annot: sham — smoky evening, beauty and memory both mournful
title: Ghazal
author: Firaq Gorakhpuri

quote: shāmeñ kisī ko māñgtī haiñ aaj bhī 'firāq' / go zindagī meñ yuuñ mujhe koī kamī nahīñ
translit: shamen kisi ko mangti hain aaj bhi 'firaq' / go zindagi mein yun mujhe koi kami nahin
translation: Evenings still yearn for someone even today, Firaq — though in life, I cannot say I truly lack for anything.
annot: sham — evenings still yearn for someone
title: Ghazal
author: Firaq Gorakhpuri

quote: thī yuuñ to shām-e-hijr magar pichhhlī raat ko / vo dard uThā 'firāq' ki maiñ muskurā diyā
translit: thi yun to sham-e-hijr magar pichli raat ko / vo dard utha 'firaq' ki main muskura diya
translation: It was merely the evening of separation — yet last night such pain arose, Firaq, that I found myself smiling.
annot: sham-e-hijr — the evening of separation, last night
title: Ghazal
author: Firaq Gorakhpuri

quote: shaam paḌte hī kisī shaḳhs kī yaad / kūcha-e-jāñ meñ sadā kartī hai
translit: sham padte hi kisi shakhs ki yaad / kucha-e-jan mein sada karti hai
translation: The moment evening falls, the memory of a certain person cries out in the lane of the soul.
annot: sham — at the first fall of evening, a certain person's memory calls out
title: Ghazal (Kulliyat)
author: Parveen Shakir

quote: shām aa.ī tirī yādoñ ke sitāre nikle / rañg hī ġham ke nahīñ naqsh bhī pyāre nikle
translit: sham aai teri yadon ke sitare nikle / rang hi gham ke nahin naqsh bhi pyare nikle
translation: Evening came and the stars of your memory appeared; not only grief's colours — even your beloved image surfaced.
annot: sham — evening arrives and memory's stars emerge
title: Kulliyat-e-Maahe Tamaam / Sadbarg (p. 86)
author: Parveen Shakir

quote: vo na aa.egā hameñ ma.alūm thā is shaam bhī / intizār us kā magar kuchh soch kar karte rahe
translit: vo na aayega hamein malum tha is sham bhi / intzar us ka magar kuchh soch kar karte rahe
translation: We knew he would not come this evening either — yet we waited for him, thinking all the same.
annot: sham — knowing he would not come this evening either, yet waiting
title: Ghazal
author: Parveen Shakir

quote: yuuñ to har shaam umīdoñ meñ guzar jaatī hai / aaj kuchh baat hai jo shaam pe ronā aayā
translit: yun to har sham ummeedon mein guzar jati hai / aaj kuchh baat hai jo sham pe rona aaya
translation: Every evening usually passes in the company of hopes — but today something made the evening bring tears.
annot: sham — every evening passes in hope; today something made me weep at evening
title: Ghazal (Filmi Shayari)
author: Shakeel Badayuni

quote: raat kyā so.e ki bāqī umr kī niiñd uḌ ga.ī / ḳhvāb kyā dekhā ki dhaḌkā lag gayā tābīr kā
translit: raat kya soye ki baqi umr ki nind ud gai / khwab kya dekha ki dhadka lag gaya tabir ka
translation: What a night it was to sleep — the sleep of all remaining life flew away; what a dream I saw — my heart trembled at its meaning.
annot: raat — one night's sleep stole the rest of a lifetime's rest
title: Ghazal
author: Ahmad Faraz

quote: shab-e-visāl hai gul kar do in charāġhoñ ko / ḳhushī kī bazm meñ kyā kām jalne vāloñ kā
translit: shab-e-visal hai gul kar do in charagon ko / khushi ki bazm mein kya kam jalne walon ka
translation: This is the night of union — extinguish these lamps; in a gathering of joy, what use are those who burn?
annot: shab-e-visal — the night of union; extinguish the lamps
title: Ghazal
author: Dagh Dehlvi

quote: gul huī jaatī hai afsurda sulagtī huī shaam
translit: gul hui jati hai afsurda sulagti hui sham
translation: The smouldering evening flickers and dies out.
annot: sham — the evening flickers and goes cold
title: Mauzu-e-Sukhan — Nuskha Hai Wafa (p. 89)
author: Faiz Ahmad Faiz

quote: kabhī to sub.h tire kunj-e-lab se ho āġhāz / kabhī to shab sar-e-kākul se mushk-bār chale
translit: kabhi to subh tire kunj-e-lab se ho aghaz / kabhi to shab sar-e-kakul se mushk-bar chale
translation: Let morning sometimes begin at the corner of your lips; let night sometimes drift down, laden with musk, from your tresses.
annot: subh / shab — sometimes let dawn begin at your lips, sometimes let night drift scented from your tresses
title: Gulon Mein Rang Bhare — Nuskha Hai Wafa (p. 264)
author: Faiz Ahmad Faiz

quote: kal chaudhvīñ kī raat thī shab bhar rahā charchā tirā / kuchh ne kahā ye chāñd hai kuchh ne kahā cheh[rā] tirā
translit: kal chaudhvin ki raat thi shab bhar raha charcha tera / kuchh ne kaha ye chand hai kuchh ne kaha chehra tera
translation: Last night was the fourteenth — the full moon night — and all night long you were the talk; some said it was the moon, some said it was your face.
annot: raat — the fourteenth night (full moon), all night long the talk was of you
title: Ghazal (Kulliyat-e-Insha / Jashn-e-Insha)
author: Ibn-e-Insha

quote: raat aa kar guzar bhī jaatī hai / ik hamārī saher nahīñ hotī
translit: raat aa kar guzar bhi jaati hai / ik hamari saher nahin hoti
translation: The night comes and passes too — only our dawn never arrives.
annot: raat / saher — the night passes, but our dawn never comes
title: Ghazal
author: Ibn-e-Insha

quote: ajnabī shaam / dhuñd chhā.ī huī hai jhīloñ par / apnī galloñ ko le ke charvāhe / sarhadī bastiyoñ meñ jā pahuñche / dil-e-nākām maiñ kahāñ jā.ūñ / ajnabī shaam maiñ kahāñ jā.ūñ
translit: ajnabi sham / dhund chha'i hui hai jhilon par / apni gallon ko le ke charvahe / sarhadi bastiyon mein ja pahunche / dil-e-nakam main kahan jaun / ajnabi sham main kahan jaun
translation: Strange evening — mist has settled over the lakes; the shepherds have led their flocks to border villages; failed heart, where shall I go — where shall I go in this strange evening?
annot: ajnabi sham — the alien evening; where shall I go in this strange dusk?
title: Ajnabi Shaam — Muntakhab Shahkar Nazmon Ka Album (p. 422)
author: Jaun Elia

quote: ishq ke sho.ale ko bhaḌkāo ki kuchh raat kaTe / dil ke añgāre ko dahkāo ki kuchh raat kaTe
translit: ishq ke shoale ko bhadkao ki kuchh raat kate / dil ke angare ko dahkao ki kuchh raat kate
translation: Fan the flame of love so that some of this night may pass; stoke the ember of the heart so that some of this night may pass.
annot: raat — stoke love's flame so that some of this night may pass
title: Ghazal
author: Makhdoom Mohiuddin

quote: raat to sārī ga.ī sunte pareshāñ-goī / 'mīr'-jī koī ghaḌī tum bhī to ārām karo
translit: raat to sari gai sunte pareshan-goi / 'mir'-ji koi ghadi tum bhi to aram karo
translation: The whole night passed listening to your anxious words — Mir, rest a while, even you.
annot: raat — the whole night passed listening to troubled speech
title: Bayaz-e-Sukhan (Abdul Shukoor Shaida)
author: Mir Taqi Mir

quote: rote phirte haiñ sārī sārī raat / ab yahī rozgār hai apnā
translit: rote phirte hain sari sari raat / ab yahi rozgar hai apna
translation: We wander weeping all night long — this has now become our occupation.
annot: raat — the whole night spent wandering and weeping; grief has become one's livelihood
title: Paiman-e-Gazal-Avval (p. 61)
author: Mir Taqi Mir

quote: bāzīcha-e-atfāl hai duniyā mere āge / hotā hai shab-o-roz tamāshā mere āge
translit: bazicha-e-atfal hai duniya mere age / hota hai shab-o-roz tamasha mere age
translation: The world is a children's game before me — night and day this spectacle plays out before me.
annot: shab-o-roz — night and day the spectacle unfolds
title: Diwan-e-Ghalib
author: Mirza Ghalib

quote: sub.h tak kaun jiyegā shab-e-tanhā.ī meñ / dil-e-nādāñ tujhe ummīd-e-sahar hai bhī to kyā
translit: sub.h tak kaun jiyega shab-e-tanha'i mein / dil-e-nadan tujhe ummid-e-sahar hai bhi to kya
translation: Who will live till morning in the night of solitude? Foolish heart, even if you hope for dawn — what use is it?
annot: shab-e-tanha'i / sahar — alone in the night, what use is hope for dawn?
title: Ghazal
author: Muztar Khairabadi

quote: maiñ sote sote ka.ī baar chauñk chauñk paḌā / tamām raat tire pahluoñ se aañch aa.ī
translit: main sote sote kai baar chaunk chaunk pada / tamam raat tere pahluwon se aanch aai
translation: Time and again I woke with a start from sleep — all night long warmth came from your side.
annot: raat — all night startling awake, the warmth of your presence
title: Ghazal
author: Nasir Kazmi

quote: nīñd āatī nahīñ to subah talak / gard-e-mahtāb kā safar dekho
translit: nind aati nahin to subah talak / gard-e-mahtab ka safar dekho
translation: If sleep will not come till morning, then watch the journey of the moon's dust.
annot: raat / subah — sleepless till morning; watch the dust of moonlight travel
title: Ghazal
author: Nasir Kazmi

quote: vo raat kā be-navā musāfir vo terā shā.ir vo terā 'nāsir' / tirī galī tak to ham ne dekhā thā phir na jaane kidhar gayā vo
translit: vo raat ka be-nava musafir vo tera sha'ir vo tera 'nasir' / teri gali tak to ham ne dekha tha phir na jane kidhar gaya vo
translation: That wayfarer of the night, your poet, your Nasir — we saw him as far as your lane; after that, who knows where he went.
annot: raat — the night's homeless traveller, the poet Nasir
title: Paiman-e-Gazal
author: Nasir Kazmi
